# Task: BibTeX Reference Generation for Papers

## Query
Please read workspace/query.md for task instructions, refer to the input materials in the context/ directory, and save all output files in the current directory.

## Deliverables
1. ref.bib - Complete BibTeX reference file

## Instructions
- Work in the current directory
- Create all required files as specified
- Ensure the code is runnable and produces the expected output

I’m going to compile the BibTeX by looking up each title across scholarly sources, then I’ll save everything into `ref.bib`. This is a fairly large list, so I’ll work through it in batches and make sure we end with one entry per paper title.

payloads.text: I’m going to compile the BibTeX by looking up each title across scholarly sources, then I’ll save everything into `ref.bib`. This is a fairly large list, so I’ll work through it in batches and make sure we end with one entry per paper title.
Web search is unavailable here, so I’m falling back to direct fetches from publisher and paper pages where possible. I’ll use Crossref/DOI and other accessible sources to build the bibliography.

I’ve identified strong Crossref matches for most titles. A few items are likely books, arXiv/RSS papers, or have title variants, so I’m now generating BibTeX from DOI where possible and filling the remaining gaps manually.

The DOI fetch route got throttled/stalled, so I’m switching to a faster method: query Crossref directly for BibTeX metadata and write the bibliography from that. This should be much more reliable here.

Good: the bibliography file is built with 61 solid entries. Four titles still need manual resolution: 1, 12, 13, and 57. I’m checking those directly now, especially because 12 and 57 look like title variants of the same paper.

I resolved 1 and 13, and I intentionally dropped 57 because it appears to be the same paper as 12 with a spelling variant (“Semi-Autonomous” vs “Semiautonomous”). That avoids duplicating the same citation under two entries. I’m doing a quick sanity check on the final file now.

Done. `ref.bib` is saved in the current directory.

Note: one listed title appears duplicated:
- [12] “The Shared Control Dynamic Window Approach for Non-Holonomic Semi-Autonomous Robots”
- [57] “The Shared Control Dynamic Window Approach for Non-Holonomic Semiautonomous Robots”

I treated that as the same paper to avoid a duplicate BibTeX entry. Also, title [13] is commonly cited as the arXiv PPO paper, so I used the standard arXiv BibTeX entry.

I couldn’t create a git commit because this workspace isn’t a git repository.
